ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for tom yum cream baked pasta specifically; closest_parent 'pasta food product' (FOODON_00002187) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for baked pasta with tom yum cream sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 'Pasta (substance)' used as closest parent; no specific concept for baked pasta with tom yum cream sauce exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of tom yum cream baked pasta is approximately 350 g, typically served as a single-portion baked dish in a cast-iron skillet or oven-proof dish.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta with cream sauce, baked — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta, cream sauce, and cheese, adjusted for tom yum paste contribution
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in any approved primary source for tom yum cream baked pasta. Values are estimated by component-weighted composition: cooked pasta (~55% by weight, USDA SR Legacy #20121), heavy cream sauce (~25%, USDA SR Legacy #01053 heavy cream + roux), mozzarella/cheddar cheese (~10%, USDA SR Legacy #01009), tom yum paste and aromatics (~10%, estimated from Thai curry paste USDA SR Legacy #02065 and lemongrass). Macros cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central baked pasta casserole entries (FDC ID 172004). Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pasta (cooked, white, boiled) GI ~47–50; cream sauce and cheese additions lower overall GI of composite dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tom yum cream baked pasta. GI estimated at 48 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being cooked white pasta (GI ~47–50 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021, Diabetes Care). The addition of cream, cheese, and tom yum paste (fat and protein-rich components) further attenuates glycemic response; composite GI estimated conservatively at 48. GL per serving = (48 × 75.25 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 36.1. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1470 mg per 350 g serving) from tom yum paste, cheese, and cream sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium load (~1470 mg per serving, representing approximately 65% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-salt preparation or choose lower-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~36 per serving) from refined pasta carbohydrates, partially offset by fat and protein from cream and cheese
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in reduced portions (150–200 g) and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to moderate the glycemic load; the cream and cheese content slows glucose absorption but the total carbohydrate load per full serving remains significant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~5.1 g/100 g; ~17.9 g per serving) from heavy cream and cheese, combined with elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high saturated fat content (~17.9 g per serving, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 20 g for many adults) and high sodium, both of which adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Cream Baked Pasta is a Singapore-originated fusion dish that combines the bold, aromatic flavours of Thai tom yum soup with the richness of a Western cream-baked pasta. The dish is prepared by cooking pasta (typically penne or fusilli) until al dente, then tossing it in a tom yum-infused cream sauce made from heavy cream, tom yum paste, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, chilli, and garlic. The sauced pasta is transferred to an oven-proof dish, topped with mozzarella or cheddar cheese, and baked until the cheese is golden and bubbling. The result is a dish with a creamy, slightly spicy, and tangy flavour profile — the sour-spicy notes of tom yum are mellowed by the richness of the cream and the savoury depth of melted cheese. Texturally, the pasta is soft and coated in a thick, luscious sauce with a slightly crisp cheese crust on top. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (~195 kcal/100 g) with moderate protein (~7.8 g/100 g), significant fat (~9.2 g/100 g, predominantly saturated from dairy), and refined carbohydrates (~21.5 g/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), but the glycemic load per full serving (~350 g) is approximately 36, which is clinically significant for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is high (~420 mg/100 g), making this dish a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is commonly served as a main course in Singapore cafes and fusion restaurants, priced at the mid-to-upper casual dining range. The dish reflects Singapore's multicultural culinary identity, blending Southeast Asian spice traditions with European pasta techniques.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for tom yum cream baked pasta exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition from USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta, heavy cream, and cheese, with cross-referencing against USDA baked pasta casserole entries. GI is analogously estimated from University of Sydney GI Database data for cooked white pasta. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries; no single approved-source entry exists for this specific fusion dish. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1: vitamin D is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation; missing-data sentinel applied per G3 policy. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1: vitamin E is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation; missing-data sentinel applied per G3 policy. 4. selenium_mcg is -1: selenium is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation; missing-data sentinel applied per G3 policy. 5. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on cooked white pasta GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; the cream and cheese components are expected to attenuate glycemic response. 6. The food_name has been normalised from 'Tom Yummy Cream Baked Pasta' (menu-style branding) to 'Tom Yum Cream Baked Pasta' (canonical English). The original menu name is preserved in aliases. 7. Halal status is listed as false because the dish may contain non-halal ingredients depending on the restaurant; Flavours by Saute - Funan is not confirmed halal-certified. 8. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on typical Singapore cafe single-portion baked pasta servings. 9. Multilingual names are descriptive translations; this dish does not have established native-language names as it is a Singapore fusion creation; Thai name included due to the tom yum component origin.
